rationale: The citing article is explicitly a comment on Novemsky and Kahneman (2005) and organizes its argument around that paper's findings and propositions. It summarizes the cited paper's three core empirical results, evaluates methodological limits of its probabilistic buying/selling and BDM-based evidence, and repeatedly discusses how far those conclusions should generalize. It then adds proposed moderators (emotional attachment and cognitive perspective) as additional boundary conditions relative to the cited framework. This is a central, theory-shaping use rather than a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper takes Novemsky and Kahneman (2005) as its main point of departure and qualifies parts of its empirical interpretation, especially regarding probabilistic transactions. It extends the boundary discussion by proposing additional psychological moderators that sharpen when loss aversion should or should not appear.
